title: Shenzhou-23 crew arrives at Tiangong as China maps path to 2030 lunar landing
summary: Three Chinese astronauts arrived at the Tiangong space station, with one set to become China's first to stay in orbit for a full year. China also outlined plans for a crewed lunar landing by 2030.

title: NASA to add missions to SpaceX commercial crew contract
summary: NASA intends to extend SpaceX's commercial crew contract by adding more missions to ensure crew transportation to the International Space Station, as Boeing's spacecraft may not be certified in time.

title: Blue Origin completes investigation into New Glenn launch failure
summary: Blue Origin has concluded the investigation into the New Glenn rocket's third flight failure, allowing launches to resume. The company is now cleared to proceed with future missions.

title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the first Starship V3 on May 22, achieving most of its planned suborbital flight test objectives.

title: Space Force awards Viasat, SES $437 million for military satellite network
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded Viasat and SES a $437 million contract to build four small geostationary satellites for the Protected Tactical Satcom-Global program.

title: NASA unveils sweeping reorganization
summary: NASA announced a major reorganization on May 22, merging mission directorates and reassigning personnel. This marks one of the agency's most significant restructurings in recent years.

title: Leveraging AUKUS and southern geography: building Australia’s dual-use space infrastructure for strategic resilience
summary: Australia is developing dual-use space infrastructure, including the Deep Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC) Site 1 in Western Australia, to enhance strategic resilience through AUKUS partnerships. Full operational capability for DARC is targeted for 2027.

title: SpaceX IPO filing casts Starlink Mobile as future wireless challenger
summary: SpaceX's IPO filing highlights Starlink Mobile as a potential competitor to terrestrial wireless networks, positioning it to challenge traditional mobile services even in urban areas. The filing suggests next-generation direct-to-smartphone capabilities that aim to match or surpass existing mobile network performance.

title: Rocket Lab launches ninth Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its ninth radar imaging satellite for Synspective. The company also plans to sell up to $3 billion in stock to fund future initiatives.

title: Rocket Lab wins first GEO satellite production contract from U.S. Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to build and operate two GEO satellites with optical payloads. This marks Rocket Lab's first GEO satellite production contract.

title: Grab the Estes Blue Origin model rocket ahead of Memorial Day with it's lowest price since January!
summary: The Estes Blue Origin New Shepard model rocket is on sale for $29.99 on Amazon, its lowest price since January. The deal is cheaper than the official Estes webstore price.

title: Lunar Strike offers a pessimistic, and worryingly realistic look at the future of space travel
summary: The article 'Lunar Strike' presents a pessimistic and realistic outlook on the future of space travel. It highlights concerns about the challenges and risks associated with lunar missions.

title: China reveals 3-person Shenzhou 23 crew, including Hong Kong's 1st astronaut
summary: China announced the three-person crew for the Shenzhou 23 mission, including Hong Kong's first astronaut. The launch is scheduled for May 24.

title: 'Rick and Morty' Season 9: Release date & how to watch Adult Swim’s zany sci-fi satire
summary: Adult Swim announced the release date for 'Rick and Morty' Season 9, continuing the zany sci-fi satire. The show explores themes of possibility and imagination.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 211 — Oysters in Space
summary: This Week In Space podcast Episode 211 features a discussion with Jacob Scoccimerra of Monolith Space about 'Oysters in Space'. The episode is hosted by Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik.

title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12: Don't miss these stunning photos from the launch of the most powerful Starship yet
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its 12th Starship flight, featuring the more powerful Starship V3 variant from Starbase. The event produced stunning launch photography as part of efforts to support future lunar astronaut missions.

title: 'For All Mankind's' Costa Ronin on ruling Mars, honoring cosmonaut history, and learning to tie his tie (interview)
summary: Costa Ronin discusses the challenges of ruling Mars in the TV series 'For All Mankind' and reflects on honoring cosmonaut history while learning to tie his tie. The interview highlights the intersection of space exploration and personal growth.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 aboard the International Space Station has been busy with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities through April. The crew has been actively engaged in various operational tasks.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to China's Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 2022. The mission marks another step in China's ongoing space station operations.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, at 5:30:21 pm CDT from SpaceX's facilities. The mission impressed observers with its performance during the inaugural flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the coming week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a key development in the future of spaceflight.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is progressing with planning and processing for Artemis III, a significant crewed mission. The mission includes key tests for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. This follows multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company continued its growth trajectory from 2025.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific details about the space industry news are provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication showcases milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring materials can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with their expertise.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This technology is opening new pathways for advancing space missions and outreach.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. However, the provided text lacks specific details about the context or company involved.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute system underwent a critical test known as 'the great parachute bake-out' to ensure its reliability under extreme conditions. This test is essential for validating the parachute's performance before a future mission.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life while adhering to strict planetary protection measures.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling improved handling and docking of spacecraft. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully passed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that current military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities within the US military.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: The ESA Chief urges Europe to double funding for space initiatives, emphasizing the need for increased investment despite recent growth in public funds. The call highlights concerns that current funding levels are insufficient for industry demands.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's recent stakeholder survey identified improved Moon technology as the top priority for future space tech investments. The survey aimed to address key technical challenges in space exploration.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: ESA has approved two Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research cost-effectively. The missions demonstrate Europe's commitment to affordable yet innovative space science.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO, expected next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system could escalate the nuclear arms race rather than protect America. The system is intended to serve as a defensive measure.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve Chinese researchers, posing potential security risks. The findings highlight concerns over collaborations with China in scientific projects.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in design reviews to enhance efficiency and reduce complexity in engineering production. The company is shifting from traditional design reviews to a more advanced 'decision intelligence' approach.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts that launch supply may fall short depending on future scenarios, with potential annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, has launched a new satellite bus business line targeting low-cost, high-power missions for communications, Earth observation, and national security programs.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March to lead the company's cloud-native and interoperable ground technology solutions amid a dynamic market. The move comes as the industry faces disruption from competitors like Starlink.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has launched a new space domain awareness software called Borealis, which is now operational six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris for the UK government.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract to build the first Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for a U.S. Space Force space domain awareness mission. This marks Rocket Lab's entry into GEO satellite manufacturing.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat has successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, showcasing messaging capabilities on Android smartphones via its L-band satellite technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is aiming to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth funding round to support its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company announced this initiative on May 21.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to identify and develop solutions to support satellite operations in orbit.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an agentic AI geospatial query system that allows users to ask questions like 'Where are new construction sites appearing in Florida?' and receive answers. This technology aims to enhance geospatial data accessibility and analysis.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its airport management tool. This collaboration aims to enhance airport operations using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX released initial financial documents ahead of a planned IPO, offering the first public glimpse into the company's financials. This filing provides unprecedented transparency into SpaceX's financial health.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: The upcoming World Radiocommunication Conference in 2027 (WRC-27) is expected to focus heavily on satellite-related agenda items, raising concerns among U.S. space operators about China's influence due to its host status.
